title: The Egerton Version of Mandeville's Travels
summary: The checked-in XML is a repository copy of University of Michigan CME item acd9576, a 2003 digital selection encoded from George F. Warner's 1889 Roxburghe Club edition. Warner transcribed the unique Egerton MS 1982 English version, supplied chapter headings and several passages from Cotton MS Titus C XVI, collated Cotton variants, and printed a French source text in parallel from Harley MS 4383 and Royal MS 20 B X with apparatus from Sloane MS 1464 and Grenville XXXIX, now Additional MS 33757. U-M retained the English text and its extensive English footnotes but explicitly omitted the French stream and the edition's front and back matter and plates; it added CORRESP pointers to the Cotton encoding and moved an Egerton-only passage from Warner's commentary into the body. A complete public scan of the 1889 edition is available through Internet Archive, but no current complete public facsimile was verified for any manuscript witness.
review_notes: The cataloged intellectual work is the distinct Middle English Egerton Version, not the physical codex, the multilingual Book of John Mandeville, Warner's bilingual volume, or U-M's digital selection.
The unique Egerton witness is undivided. Warner imposed thirty-four chapters and headings from Cotton, closely following the Harley/Royal French text; U-M serializes those editorial divisions.
French is present in Warner's source and apparatus layers but was explicitly omitted from U-M and is not treated as a primary language of the selected Egerton text.
Cotton contributed headings, five clearly identifiable supplied passages, and variants. The XML's fifty-six SUPPLIED spans are not all Cotton-derived.
The current U-M No Copyright-United States statement and the restrictive embedded 2003 availability notice are preserved as separate claims rather than silently reconciled.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for acd9576
entities.description: Repository XML supplied through the pinned CME submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item acd9576
entities.description: A 2003 electronic selection from Warner's 1889 edition. It encodes the Egerton-base English text and English notes, omits the parallel French text and front and back matter, and supplies pointers to U-M's Cotton-Version divisions.
notes: The item header was created by hand from the text and a MARC record of a microfilm copy on 26 March 2003; no item-specific production statement identifies keying, OCR, or correction methods.
The language declaration names Latin and Greek but omits overwhelmingly primary Middle English.
The selected XML contains 580 NOTE1 elements, most of them Warner's English footnotes, and 56 SUPPLIED spans for omissions or damage.
Thirty-four Greek alphabet nodes contain GAP rather than the printed glyphs.

entities.label: Warner's 1889 edition, The Buke of John Maundeuill
entities.description: A large-paper bilingual scholarly edition: the Egerton English version and a reconstructed French source text appear in parallel on pp. 1-156, followed by commentary and index; the volume also contains an introduction, glossary, and twenty-eight manuscript-miniature plates. Warner used Egerton MS 1982 as the English base, Cotton Titus C XVI for English headings, explicit supplies, and variants, Harley MS 4383 and Royal MS 20 B X for the French base, and Sloane MS 1464 and Grenville XXXIX for French variants.

bibliographic.citation: George F. Warner, ed., The Buke of John Maundeuill, Being the Travels of Sir John Mandeville, Knight, 1322-1356: A Hitherto Unpublished English Version from the Unique Copy (Egerton MS. 1982) in the British Museum, Roxburghe Club Publications 119 (Westminster: Nichols and Sons, 1889).
bibliographic.title: The Buke of John Maundeuill

notes: The title's 1322-1356 dates follow the common version; the Egerton prologue instead claims departure in 1332.
Warner's French text is subsidiary source reconstruction and apparatus, not a second work selected by U-M.
Warner moved an Egerton-only passage about Thule and Saint Thomas of Canterbury from the running text to commentary p. 220.

entities.label: Google and Internet Archive scan of Warner's 1889 edition
entities.description: A complete 360-image digital scan of the 1889 volume, digitized by Google and distributed through Internet Archive with PDF and generated derivatives.
entities.reproduction_format: 360 scan images; downloadable PDF, OCR text, and generated derivatives
notes: Verified PDF size: 59,494,131 bytes; media type: application/pdf; SHA-256: cc523953838cc0bf9bf382eb00ed9e1dee57b127acdf5b897a6f6e60965496bc.

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright
entities.description: The April 2026 revision of the Copyright Office's official duration guidance, used only to support this manifest's United States rights assessment for Warner's 1889 publication; it is not part of the textual transmission lineage.

entities.label: British Library Egerton MS 1982
entities.description: The unique manuscript witness of the Egerton Middle English version. Warner describes an undivided text copied in one northern hand on 129 leaves; modern scholarship dates the manuscript around 1400. Marginal place names are irregular or misplaced and were omitted from Warner's running edition.

holding.notes: Held at the British Library, London; current record offers physical request but no digitized content.

physical_description.extent: 129 leaves according to Warner; the encoded work has intended folio-side markers from approximately 3r to 132v
notes: Warner reports one hand and no chapter divisions.

The British Library records flyleaf provenance claims involving St Albans, Caxton, Tottel, Tuthill, and Hill; Warner explicitly doubted the Caxton claim because its alleged supporting note was lost, so these are not treated as established transmission facts.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Titus C XVI
entities.description: The unique manuscript witness of the Cotton Middle English version. Warner used it for chapter headings, five clearly identifiable supplied passages, and English variants; U-M linked Egerton divisions to corresponding divisions of its Cotton encoding.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: first quarter of the fifteenth century, 1400-1424

notes: The codex is physically extant and cataloged as intact, while Warner reports a textual lacuna equivalent to three lost leaves after fol. 53; these are different claims.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 4383
entities.description: A late-fourteenth-century French Mandeville witness containing the work at fols. 1r-46v. Warner made it the base of his parallel French text through print p. 103, where a deficiency required switching to Royal MS 20 B X.

holding.notes: The exact current British Library online record was not recovered; Biblissima's Jonas-derived authority record supplies the shelfmark, content, date, and holding institution.

notes: Biblissima records two original blocks and a Devon origin note associated with fol. 58.

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 20 B X
entities.description: An early-fifteenth-century French Mandeville manuscript. Warner used it occasionally for French readings before print p. 103 and then as the French base from p. 103, corresponding to manuscript fol. 56v, because Harley MS 4383 is deficient.

notes: The current British Library description explicitly records Warner's switch to this manuscript at p. 103 / fol. 56v.

entities.label: British Library Sloane MS 1464
entities.description: A fifteenth-century French Mandeville manuscript used for variants in Warner's French apparatus, not as a direct source for the U-M English selection.

notes: A Saint Alban note occupies fols. 161v-164.

entities.label: British Library Additional MS 33757
entities.description: A late-fourteenth-century French Mandeville manuscript used for variants in Warner's French apparatus. The current shelfmark is securely crosswalked from Grenville XXXIX because the British Library record itself names the former designation and Warner's partial collation.

notes: The current record places production in France.

entities.label: British Library Additional MS 24189
entities.description: A textless cycle of twenty-eight full-page Mandeville drawings, now dated about 1410-1420 and attributed to the Master of Mandeville Travels in Bohemia. Warner reproduced the complete miniature cycle as twenty-eight plates, but not a complete page or codex facsimile.

physical_description.extent: 16 folios plus modern leaves; 28 full-page drawings
physical_description.format: Illustrated manuscript fragment or picture-book cycle without accompanying text
notes: Current British Library attribution to Bohemia and a Czech Mandeville source supersedes Warner's older Flemish or Liege conjecture.

entities.label: Warner's twenty-eight plates from Additional MS 24189
entities.description: The complete set of twenty-eight miniatures from Additional MS 24189 reproduced as printed plates in the 1889 edition; complete as an image cycle but not a complete codex or page facsimile.
entities.reproduction_format: Twenty-eight monochrome printed plates, now visible in digital scans of the edition

entities.label: The Egerton Version of the Book of John Mandeville
entities.description: The abstract Middle English version represented uniquely by Egerton MS 1982. Seymour describes it as conflating a lost Defective-Version manuscript and a lost Middle English translation of the Anglo-Latin Royal Version.

entities.label: Lost Defective-Version source behind the Egerton Version
entities.description: A reported lost manuscript or textual intermediary of the Defective Version posited by Seymour as one source strand behind the Egerton Version; no physical exemplar is identified.

entities.label: Lost Middle English translation of the Anglo-Latin Royal Version
entities.description: A reported lost Middle English translation posited by Seymour as the second source strand behind the Egerton Version; no physical exemplar is identified.

entities.label: The Anglo-Latin Royal Version of the Book of John Mandeville
entities.description: An Anglo-Latin version tradition represented indirectly in the Egerton Version through a posited lost Middle English translation.

entities.label: The Book of John Mandeville
entities.description: The anonymous or pseudonymous fourteenth-century multilingual compilation and multiversion tradition of which the Egerton, Cotton, Defective, and Anglo-Latin Royal versions are manifestations.

assertion.notes: Identity is established by the UMDL identifier, title, source statement, and content; a separately downloadable current U-M source XML was not checksummed.

subject.description: Selected English electronic text and retained English footnotes

scope.notes: The French parallel, preliminaries, commentary except the relocated passage, glossary, index, and twenty-eight plates are omitted.

subject.description: Repository work and its linked manifest rights analysis

scope.notes: This is an evidentiary rights-analysis dependency, not a textual derivation edge.
Warner omitted irregular marginal place notes, introduced Cotton-derived chapters, placed one Egerton-only passage in commentary, and incorporated explicit Cotton supplies.

subject.description: Chapter headings and explicit Cotton-supplied English passages

scope.notes: Four XML supplies have a SOURCE attribute naming Cot or cot, and a fifth porcupine passage is identified by Warner's note as from C; the other SUPPLIED spans are not assigned to Cotton without evidence.
Cotton is a comparison witness and occasional supply source, not Warner's base English text.
Harley's deficiency at print p. 103 caused Warner to switch to Royal 20 B X.

subject.description: French apparatus before the base-text switch

scope.notes: This comparison role precedes Royal's direct base-text role from p. 103.
Apparatus-only use; this witness does not supply U-M's selected English text.
Apparatus-only use; this witness does not supply U-M's selected English text.

object.description: All twenty-eight full-page miniatures, without complete codex pages or modern leaves

scope.notes: Complete miniature-set reproduction does not equal complete manuscript facsimile.

subject.description: Mandeville text in the unique manuscript

scope.notes: A reconstructed textual derivation, not a securely identified surviving physical exemplar.

object.description: Posited Middle English Royal-Version source strand

scope.notes: A reconstructed textual derivation, not a securely identified surviving physical exemplar.
This contextual edge does not imply that Cotton is the base of Egerton; Warner used it selectively in the edition.

rights.component: Warner's 1889 transcription, French text, introduction, apparatus, commentary, index, and published plates
rights.basis: The edition was first published in 1889, U-M currently labels its selected materials public domain in the United States, and Internet Archive applies the Public Domain Mark to the complete scan. Copyright Office Circular 15A describes a 95-year maximum term for works already under statutory protection before 1978; the publication is now more than 95 years old.

rights.component: Materials presented in U-M CME item acd9576
rights.basis: The current U-M item page states that the materials are in the public domain and applies No Copyright-United States.
notes: The embedded 2003 notice permits personal, research, and teaching copies with the notice and permits linking, but expressly forbids redistribution by commercial or not-for-profit content providers; whether the current statement supersedes that restriction is unresolved.

rights.component: The local U-M-derived XML markup and transcription
rights.basis: The underlying 1889 edition and current U-M item are public domain in the United States, while the checked-in XML embeds an express redistribution restriction.

rights.component: Internet Archive PDF and generated digital derivatives of the Google scan
rights.basis: Internet Archive's item metadata applies the Creative Commons Public Domain Mark 1.0 to the item; the underlying 1889 edition is public domain in the United States.

rights.component: Twenty-eight plates as published in 1889 and reproduced in the Internet Archive scan
rights.jurisdiction: United States and item-specific provider terms
rights.basis: The plates were published in the 1889 public-domain edition. Access to their modern scan remains subject to Internet Archive's provider terms and item statement.

rights.component: Modern photographs or other reproductions supplied by British Library Imaging Services
rights.basis: No reusable item-specific image set or license was verified. British Library Imaging Services requires permission for reproduction of supplied images, independently of underlying medieval textual status.

rights.basis: No reusable item-specific image set or license was verified; British Library image-reproduction permission and service terms apply to supplied images.

editorial_practices.source_feature: An undivided Egerton text with irregular or misplaced marginal place names
editorial_practices.representation: Thirty-four numbered chapters and headings taken from Cotton and closely following Harley/Royal French headings; marginal place names omitted
editorial_practices.description: Warner imposed navigational structure from comparison traditions rather than transcribing intrinsic Egerton divisions.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Egerton base text with omissions, damage, and differences from Cotton
editorial_practices.representation: Egerton transcription with Cotton variants in English footnotes and a small number of explicit Cotton headings and supplied passages
editorial_practices.description: Warner's English stream is an edited Egerton transcription, not a diplomatic single-witness reproduction and not a conflated Cotton base text.
editorial_practices.scope: English text and English apparatus pp. 1-156
notes: Only five supplied passages are clearly attributable to Cotton; the XML's fifty-six SUPPLIED spans include many small editorial restorations for scribal omission or manuscript damage.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Multiple French witnesses related to the Egerton version
editorial_practices.representation: Harley base through p. 103, Royal base thereafter, with Royal occasional earlier readings and Sloane/Grenville variants in a separate French apparatus
editorial_practices.description: Warner prints a subsidiary parallel French reconstruction to demonstrate the English version's relationship to its source tradition.
editorial_practices.scope: French lower-page stream and French apparatus pp. 1-156

editorial_practices.source_feature: An Egerton-only passage on Thule and Saint Thomas of Canterbury within the manuscript sequence
editorial_practices.representation: Omitted from the parallel running text and printed in commentary p. 220
editorial_practices.description: Warner separates the unique passage from his running English text; U-M later reverses that placement.
editorial_practices.scope: End of the English text and commentary p. 220

editorial_practices.source_feature: A bilingual volume with preliminaries, English and French parallel texts and apparatus, commentary, index, and twenty-eight plates
editorial_practices.representation: English text and English footnotes retained; French text and its apparatus, front matter, back matter, and plates omitted
editorial_practices.description: The electronic item is a selected English work-level encoding rather than a complete digital edition of Warner's volume.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Warner's imposed Egerton chapters and a separately encoded Cotton Version
editorial_practices.representation: CORRESP attributes link every Egerton DIV1 to one or more Cotton divisions
editorial_practices.description: The links serialize chapter-level correspondence rather than claiming that Cotton is the direct source of the Egerton text.
notes: Eg.Ch.31 points to Cot.Ch.30, duplicating Eg.Ch.29's target and skipping Cot.Ch.32; this appears anomalous and is not silently corrected.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Egerton-only passage printed by Warner in commentary p. 220 rather than in the running text
editorial_practices.representation: Inserted into the body at Eg.Ch.33 with a DR note explaining the move
editorial_practices.description: U-M changes Warner's placement to restore the passage to its manuscript sequence.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Printed Greek alphabet glyphs in Warner's edition
editorial_practices.representation: Thirty-four empty Greek-language FOREIGN nodes containing GAP elements
editorial_practices.description: The encoding marks but does not transcribe the Greek alphabet glyphs; four other Greek-language quotations survive in transliteration.
editorial_practices.scope: Greek-language spans in the encoded English text

editorial_practices.source_feature: Warner print pagination and Egerton foliation
editorial_practices.representation: PB markers reproduce English-text pages 1-156 and MILESTONE markers record intended manuscript folio sides; two UNIT values are malformed as f.40 and f.70
editorial_practices.description: The encoding preserves print and manuscript navigation as representation metadata, not intrinsic work divisions.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/acd9576.xml, header, editorial declaration, and complete selected text.
evidence.summary: Supplies the U-M item identity, 2003 date, Warner source, explicit omission of French and front/back matter, Cotton CORRESP links, embedded provider terms, English text, notes, supplies, and passage-placement intervention.

evidence.citation: Computational and direct structural inspection of General Corpus acd9576.xml.
evidence.locator: DIV1, P, PB, MILESTONE, NOTE1, SUPPLIED, CORRESP, HEAD, FOREIGN, Q1, LANG, and SOURCE attributes
evidence.summary: Finds 35 top-level prose divisions, 214 body paragraphs, print pages 1-156, 257 intended folio markers, 580 notes, 56 supplied spans, all chapter correspondence targets, five clearly Cotton-derived supplied passages, and Greek glyph gaps. Counts exclude nested apparatus from the work's one-item prose structure.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Mandeville's Travels: The Egerton Version, item acd9576.
evidence.locator: Item information, contents, rights statement, and full-text access
evidence.summary: Confirms the public digital item, 2003 publication, Warner source, thirty-four displayed chapters, stable handle, and current Public Domain / No Copyright-United States assertion.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Cotton Version of Mandeville's Travels, item aeh6691.
evidence.locator: Table of contents and division identifiers
evidence.summary: Confirms the separately encoded Cotton prologue and thirty-five chapters targeted by acd9576 CORRESP values, supporting detection of the duplicate Eg.Ch.31 to Cot.Ch.30 mapping and skipped Cotton chapter 32.

evidence.citation: George F. Warner, ed., The Buke of John Maundeuill, Roxburghe Club Publications 119 (Westminster: Nichols and Sons, 1889), Internet Archive thebukeofjohnmaundeuill.
evidence.locator: Title page; introduction pp. v-xlvi; glossary; parallel text pp. 1-156; commentary pp. 157-220; index through p. 232; twenty-eight plates
evidence.summary: The inspected scan establishes all base-witness, collation, chaptering, omission, parallel-text, passage-relocation, commentary, and plate claims and supports the edition-to-witness relation scopes.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item thebukeofjohnmaundeuill and item metadata.
evidence.summary: Identifies a Google-digitized copy uploaded on 25 January 2025, exposes the direct 59,494,131-byte PDF and derivatives, and applies the Creative Commons Public Domain Mark 1.0. The verified PDF SHA-256 is cc523953838cc0bf9bf382eb00ed9e1dee57b127acdf5b897a6f6e60965496bc.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library catalog, The Buke of John Maundeuill, record 37371.
evidence.summary: Confirms publication, Roxburghe series number, large-paper physical extent, and twenty-eight miniatures reproduced from Additional MS 24189.
notes: Its older Jean d'Outremeuse authorship heading is not treated as resolved modern authorship evidence.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Egerton MS 1982.
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, vellum support, fifteenth-century range, English language, legendary-author cataloging, physical-request conditions, and no current digitized content; it also records attributed flyleaf provenance claims.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Cotton MS Titus C XVI.
evidence.summary: Confirms the Cotton Version's shelfmark, first-quarter fifteenth-century English production, 133-folio physical description, current binding, physical requestability, and absence of a current public image link.

evidence.citation: Biblissima, London, British Library, Harley MS 4383, Biblissima+ Q272640, data from Jonas.
evidence.locator: Presentation, contents, notes, and data-source sections
evidence.summary: Confirms the shelfmark and holding institution, dates the manuscript 1367-1399, locates Mandeville at fols. 1r-46v and model letters at fols. 48r-57v, and records two original blocks and a Devon origin note. Direct page inspection found no item-specific IIIF manifest or public image surrogate.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Royal MS 20 B X.
evidence.summary: Confirms the early-fifteenth-century, 85-folio vellum manuscript and explicitly records Warner's use from print p. 103 / fol. 56v to supply Harley's deficiency.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Sloane MS 1464.
evidence.summary: Confirms a fifteenth-century vellum quarto of 164 folios, with French Mandeville at fols. 1-161 and a Saint Alban note at fols. 161v-164; no current digitized content is linked.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Additional MS 33757.
evidence.summary: Confirms the secure crosswalk from Grenville XXXIX, Warner's partial collation, French Mandeville content, late-fourteenth-century French production, 75 vellum folios, and no current digitized content.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Additional MS 24189.
evidence.summary: Confirms twenty-eight textless full-page tinted grisaille Mandeville drawings on green parchment, current c. 1410-1420 Bohemian attribution and Czech-source relation, sixteen-folio extent plus modern leaves, restricted physical access, and an unavailable legacy digital-content link.

evidence.citation: M. C. Seymour, ed., The Egerton Version of Mandeville's Travels, EETS Original Series 336 (Oxford University Press, 2010), Folger record 263094.
evidence.summary: Confirms the unique manuscript written around 1400 and reports Seymour's reconstruction of the version as conflating a lost Defective-Version manuscript with a lost Middle English translation of the Anglo-Latin Royal Version.

evidence.citation: The Book of John Mandeville, scholarly introduction, Middle English Texts Series Editions.
evidence.summary: Synthesizes scholarship on the unresolved author persona, debated French origin and c. 1357 date, compiled sources, multiversion transmission, unique Cotton and Egerton versions, and travel, pilgrimage, geography, and marvels genres.

evidence.citation: Middle English Grammar Corpus / LALME manuscript sample L0457 for British Library Egerton MS 1982.
evidence.summary: Localizes the Egerton witness's scribal language to the North Riding of Yorkshire and associates the sample with a hand around 1400.
notes: The item-level PDF at the official University of Stavanger URL was indexed and discoverable but returned HTTP 403 to automated retrieval; it was not treated as a verified downloadable deliverable or cached.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation services.
evidence.summary: Explains item-specific paid photography for original manuscripts, exact shelfmark and folio requirements, conservation and rights restrictions, and separate reproduction-permission needs.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms and conditions, updated January 2026.
evidence.summary: States the current service, delivery, payment, image-use, and reproduction-permission terms governing images supplied by British Library Imaging Services.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright, revised April 2026.
evidence.summary: Describes the 95-year maximum total term for works already under federal statutory protection before 1978 and states the pre-1931 rule for works published in the United States. It is used together with Warner's 1889 publication date and the item-specific U-M and Internet Archive public-domain assertions, not as proof that the Westminster publication was first published in the United States.

open_questions.question: Does U-M's current No Copyright-United States statement supersede the embedded 2003 restriction against redistribution by commercial or not-for-profit content providers?
next_steps: Obtain written interpretation from U-M before redistributing the XML or a close derivative.
Continue to distinguish copyright status from provider-contract status.

open_questions.question: Do complete digital surrogates of Egerton MS 1982, Cotton Titus C XVI, Harley 4383, Royal 20 B X, Sloane 1464, Additional 33757, or Additional 24189 exist behind unavailable legacy services or by mediated request, and what reuse terms apply?
next_steps: Ask the British Library by exact shelfmark about existing photography or microfilm, completeness, delivery, cost, and item-specific license terms.
Do not treat Warner's edition scan or Additional 24189 plates as a complete manuscript facsimile.
Recheck restored British Library digitized-manuscript services and IIIF endpoints.

open_questions.question: How was U-M item acd9576 keyed, OCRed, corrected, and quality-controlled from the Warner source?
next_steps: Seek project-level or item-level production documentation from U-M.
Do not infer OCR or manual keying from the header's statement that it was created from text and a MARC record of microfilm.

open_questions.question: Is Eg.Ch.31's CORRESP value Cot.Ch.30 an encoding error, and should it instead target Cotton chapter 32 or another division?
next_steps: Compare the full Warner and U-M Egerton/Cotton chapter content and inspect original project mapping documentation.
Do not silently change the source XML mapping; preserve the observed duplicate and missing target in the manifest.

open_questions.question: How securely can the Egerton Version's two posited lost source strands be reconstructed and related to extant Defective and Anglo-Latin Royal witnesses?
next_steps: Review Seymour's full 2010 introduction and apparatus and subsequent stemmatic scholarship.
Retain lost intermediaries as supported scholarly reconstructions, not identified physical manuscripts.

open_questions.question: What is the current British Library catalog identifier and full physical description for Harley MS 4383 after service restoration?
next_steps: Search the restored British Library catalog by exact shelfmark or request a curatorial record.
Use Biblissima's shelfmark/date/content record without inventing physical details in the meantime.
